metadata.database_title: MycoDiscovery Target Explorer: Target-First Mycobacterial Drug Discovery Database
metadata.structure: category -> target -> published compound -> resistance mutation (each mutation individually cited)
taxonomy_corrections: GuaB2 moved from 'Central Metabolism' to 'DNA/RNA Information Processing & Nucleotide Metabolism' — it supplies nucleotide precursors, not energy metabolism.
PanK, RibBA, DHPS, BioA/BioD split out of 'Amino Acid Biosynthesis' into a new 'Cofactor & Vitamin Biosynthesis' category — none of these produce amino acids.

LipB moved from 'Virulence & Host Adaptation' to 'Central Metabolism' (cross-referenced in Cofactor & Vitamin Biosynthesis) — it attaches cofactors to metabolic enzyme complexes, not a virulence factor.
UbiA function description corrected: in M. tuberculosis this is DPPR synthase (cell-wall precursor), not a menaquinone-synthesis enzyme; that role belongs to MenA (already correctly categorized).
metadata.curation_note: Cell Wall & Envelope Biosynthesis category: 5 of 9 targets (DprE1/E2, InhA, EmbCAB, MmpL3, Pks13) are literature-verified with real published compounds, resistance mutations, and citations. Remaining targets in this category and all targets in the other 7 categories are scaffolded (function/significance filled in, compound list empty) pending the same literature-curation pass.

targets.name: DprE1/E2
targets.significance: Essential; one of the most extensively pursued TB drug targets — over a dozen distinct chemical scaffolds identified as inhibitors.

compounds.name: BTZ043
compounds.mechanism: Covalent nitroso-adduct formation at Cys387 of DprE1, blocking arabinogalactan precursor synthesis.

mutations.citation: Trefzer C et al., Characterization of DprE1-mediated benzothiazinone resistance in M. tuberculosis. Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2016;60(11):6451-6459. PMC5075049
Trefzer C et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2016;60(11):6451-6459. PMC5075049
Trefzer C et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2016;60(11):6451-6459. PMC5075049
Trefzer C et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2016;60(11):6451-6459. PMC5075049
Trefzer C et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2016;60(11):6451-6459. PMC5075049

mutations.effect: Low-level cross-resistance to BTZ043 (~1.7-fold IC50 shift) via MmpS5-MmpL5 efflux upregulation
mutations.citation: Poulton NC et al., Mutations in rv0678 confer low-level resistance to benzothiazinone DprE1 inhibitors in M. tuberculosis. bioRxiv 2022.06.29.498178; PubMed 35920665

compounds.name: PBTZ169 (Macozinone)
compounds.mechanism: Highly potent covalent DprE1 inhibitor via the same Cys387 nitroso-adduct mechanism as BTZ043.

mutations.citation: Trefzer C et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2016;60(11):6451-6459. PMC5075049

mutations.effect: Low-level cross-resistance (~2.4-4-fold IC50 shift) via efflux pump upregulation
mutations.citation: Poulton NC et al., bioRxiv 2022.06.29.498178; PubMed 35920665

compounds.name: TBA-7371
compounds.mechanism: Non-covalent DprE1 inhibitor, structurally distinct from the benzothiazinones.

mutations.effect: Reduced susceptibility, though less pronounced than for BTZ043/PBTZ169/bedaquiline
mutations.citation: Poulton NC et al., Mutations in Rv0678 Reduce Susceptibility of M. tuberculosis to the DprE1 Inhibitor TBA-7371. Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2023. doi:10.1128/aac.00052-23

compounds.name: OPC-167832
compounds.phase: Phase II clinical trial (part of novel regimens)
compounds.mechanism: Covalent DprE1 inhibitor of a distinct chemotype from benzothiazinones.

mutations.citation: Poulton NC et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2023. doi:10.1128/aac.00052-23

targets.name: InhA
targets.function: Mycolic acid synthesis (enoyl-ACP reductase, FAS-II elongation cycle)
targets.significance: Target of isoniazid (after KatG pro-drug activation) and of a growing class of direct InhA inhibitors designed to bypass KatG-mediated resistance.

compounds.name: Isoniazid (INH)
compounds.mechanism: Activated by KatG to form an INH-NAD adduct that inhibits InhA; requires functional KatG.

mutations.effect: High-level INH resistance (most common clinical mutation, loss of pro-drug activation)
mutations.citation: WHO Catalogue of mutations in M. tuberculosis complex, 2nd ed., 2023

mutations.effect: Low-level INH resistance via InhA overexpression
mutations.citation: WHO Catalogue of mutations in M. tuberculosis complex, 2nd ed., 2023

compounds.name: NITD-916
compounds.mechanism: Binds directly to InhA in an NADH-dependent manner, blocking the enoyl-substrate pocket without requiring KatG activation.

mutations.effect: Resistance to NITD-916; not cross-resistant to isoniazid
mutations.citation: Manjunatha UH et al., Direct inhibitors of InhA are active against M. tuberculosis. Sci Transl Med. 2015; PMC4383039

mutations.effect: Resistance to NITD-916; not cross-resistant to isoniazid
mutations.citation: Manjunatha UH et al., Sci Transl Med. 2015; PMC4383039

mutations.effect: Resistance to NITD-916; not cross-resistant to isoniazid
mutations.citation: Manjunatha UH et al., Sci Transl Med. 2015; PMC4383039
Manjunatha UH et al., Sci Transl Med. 2015; PMC4383039
Manjunatha UH et al., Sci Transl Med. 2015; PMC4383039
Manjunatha UH et al., Sci Transl Med. 2015; PMC4383039

mutations.effect: Resistance to NITD-916; homologous to but not identical in effect to isoniazid promoter mutations
mutations.citation: Xu M et al., Mechanisms of resistance against NITD-916, a direct inhibitor of M. tuberculosis InhA. Tuberculosis. 2017;107:133-136. PMC5679704

targets.name: EmbCAB
targets.function: Arabinosyltransferases responsible for arabinan polymerization in arabinogalactan and lipoarabinomannan
targets.significance: Target of ethambutol; first-line companion drug in standard TB regimens.

compounds.name: Ethambutol (EMB)
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits arabinosyltransferases (EmbA/EmbB/EmbC), blocking arabinan polymerization in the cell wall.

mutations.effect: High-level EMB resistance (most frequent clinical mutation)
mutations.citation: WHO Catalogue of mutations in M. tuberculosis complex, 2nd ed., 2023
WHO Catalogue of mutations in M. tuberculosis complex, 2nd ed., 2023
WHO Catalogue of mutations in M. tuberculosis complex, 2nd ed., 2023

targets.name: MmpL3
targets.function: Proton-motive-force-driven transporter of trehalose monomycolate (TMM) across the plasma membrane for mycolic acid attachment to the cell wall core
targets.significance: One of the most heavily pursued targets in current TB drug discovery — hit by at least 6 structurally distinct chemical series.

compounds.name: SQ109
compounds.mechanism: Allosterically blocks TMM translocation through MmpL3, distinct from EMB's mechanism despite structural similarity.

mutations.effect: Resistance (base mutation, potentiates further resistance when combined with second-site mutations)
mutations.citation: McNeil MB et al., Multiple mutations in M. tuberculosis MmpL3 increase resistance to MmpL3 inhibitors. mSphere. 2020;5:e00985-20. PMC7565900
McNeil MB et al., mSphere. 2020;5:e00985-20. PMC7565900
McNeil MB et al., mSphere. 2020;5:e00985-20. PMC7565900

mutations.effect: Cross-resistance to SQ109 (from adamantyl urea resistant isolates)
mutations.citation: Li W et al., Novel Insights into the Mechanism of Inhibition of MmpL3. Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2014. PMC4249373
Li W et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 2014. PMC4249373

compounds.name: AU1235
compounds.mechanism: Binds the MmpL3 substrate-translocation channel; shares a binding region with SQ109.

mutations.effect: No significant change in AU1235 susceptibility despite SQ109 cross-resistance (compound-specific resistance profile)
mutations.citation: McNeil MB et al., mSphere. 2020;5:e00985-20. PMC7565900

compounds.name: IDR-0334448 / IDR-0033216
compounds.mechanism: MmpL3 inhibitors identified via chemical-genetic profiling of resistant mutant isolation.

mutations.citation: McNeil MB et al., mSphere. 2020;5:e00985-20. PMC7565900

mutations.effect: Contributes to high-level resistance in combination with F255L
mutations.citation: McNeil MB et al., mSphere. 2020;5:e00985-20. PMC7565900

targets.name: Pks13
targets.function: Polyketide synthase catalyzing the terminal Claisen-type condensation step of mycolic acid biosynthesis; thioesterase (TE) domain releases the mature mycolic acid precursor
targets.significance: Validated, essential, and clinically unencumbered target — no pre-existing clinical resistance since no marketed drug yet targets it directly.

compounds.name: TAM16
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits the thioesterase (TE) domain of Pks13, blocking release of the mature mycolic acid precursor.

mutations.citation: Aggarwal A et al., Development of a Novel Lead that Targets M. tuberculosis Polyketide Synthase 13. Cell. 2017;170(2):249-259. RCSB 5V3Z
Aggarwal A et al., Cell. 2017;170(2):249-259
Aggarwal A et al., Cell. 2017;170(2):249-259
bioRxiv 2026 chemical-genetic interaction profiling study (preprint, doi pending formal publication)

compounds.name: Compound TP2
compounds.mechanism: Targets the N-terminal acyl carrier protein (N-ACP) domain of Pks13, distinct from the TE domain targeted by TAM16.

mutations.effect: Resistance to TP2 (N-ACP domain mutation, no cross-resistance to TAM16)
mutations.citation: Bergeret F et al. cited in: Optimization of TAM16... J Med Chem. PMC8762665

compounds.name: X20404 (DNA-encoded library hit)
compounds.mechanism: Binds the Pks13-TE substrate-accommodating channel, near catalytic Ser1533.

mutations.effect: Resistance to X20404, characterized structurally by X-ray co-crystallization
mutations.citation: Inhibitors of the Thioesterase Activity of M. tuberculosis Pks13 Discovered Using DNA-Encoded Chemical Library Screening. PubMed 38577994; RCSB 8TR4

targets.name: MurX/MraY
targets.function: Phospho-MurNAc-pentapeptide translocase — first membrane step of peptidoglycan synthesis
targets.significance: Validated essential target in other bacteria (e.g. tunicamycin-class inhibitors); mycobacterial-specific inhibitor/resistance literature is comparatively sparse.

targets.name: UbiA
targets.function: Decaprenylphosphoryl-ribose (DPPR) synthase — produces the decaprenylphosphate carrier lipid for arabinogalactan/lipoarabinomannan precursor synthesis
targets.significance: Essential for cell wall precursor supply; distinct from menaquinone-synthesis prenyltransferases despite family name similarity.

targets.name: AccD6
targets.function: Beta-subunit of an acyl-CoA carboxylase (ACCase) complex feeding long-chain fatty acid/mycolic acid precursor synthesis
targets.significance: Essential component of a validated multi-subunit fatty acid carboxylation complex.

targets.name: FAS-I / FAS-II
targets.gene_locus: Rv2524c (fas1); FAS-II is a multi-enzyme system incl. InhA, KasA, KasB, MabA
targets.function: Fatty acid synthase systems producing the C16-C26 (FAS-I) and very-long-chain meromycolate (FAS-II) precursors of mycolic acids
targets.significance: Core biosynthetic machinery for mycolic acids; several FAS-II components (InhA, KasA) are independently validated drug targets, catalogued separately in this database.

targets.name: BlaC
targets.significance: Primary intrinsic resistance mechanism against most beta-lactams; inhibited by clavulanate, enabling carbapenem/amoxicillin-clavulanate combinations.

targets.name: PonA
targets.significance: Major target for beta-lactam binding/inhibition; involved in peptidoglycan transpeptidation.

targets.name: PbpB
targets.significance: Essential for septation; sensitive to specific carbapenems.

targets.name: RipA
targets.significance: Required for cell division and reported beta-lactam synergy.

targets.name: LdtMt1/Mt2/Mt3
targets.significance: Catalyze 3-3 peptidoglycan cross-links (non-classical, penicillin-insensitive route); key contributor to intrinsic beta-lactam resistance in non-replicating bacilli.

targets.name: ATP Synthase
targets.significance: Bedaquiline target; critical energy-generation node, validated by a marketed drug.

targets.name: NADH-2 (Ndh)
targets.significance: Respiratory chain entry point; bypasses Type I NADH dehydrogenase, relevant to clofazimine/bedaquiline mechanism synergy.

targets.name: Cyt bc1-aa3
targets.significance: Terminal respiratory oxidase; critical for aerobic survival, target of Q203/telacebec (imidazopyridine class).

targets.name: ICL (Icl1/Icl2)
targets.significance: Essential for fatty-acid/lipid utilization during persistence and dormancy.

targets.name: PEPCK (PckA)
targets.significance: Critical for survival on host-derived fatty acids/cholesterol during infection.

targets.name: MenA/MenD
targets.significance: Essential for the electron transport chain; distinct from UbiA/cell-wall prenylation.

targets.name: LipB
targets.significance: Attaches lipoic acid cofactor to central metabolic enzyme complexes (e.g. pyruvate/2-oxoglutarate dehydrogenase); reclassified here from 'virulence' since its role is cofactor-attachment to metabolic machinery, not a direct virulence factor.

targets.name: DNA Gyrase
targets.significance: Target of fluoroquinolones; extensively characterized WHO-catalogued resistance mutations.

targets.name: RNA Polymerase
targets.significance: Target of rifamycins; rpoB S450L is the single most common MDR-TB-defining mutation worldwide.

targets.name: DnaE1
targets.function: Catalytic alpha-subunit of DNA polymerase III
targets.significance: Catalytic core of chromosomal DNA replication; validated by griselimycin-class inhibitors.

targets.name: ThyA
targets.significance: Essential for dTMP/DNA precursor supply; para-aminosalicylic acid (PAS) resistance also implicates this pathway via folate metabolism.

targets.name: NrdEF2
targets.significance: Essential for maintaining balanced dNTP pools for DNA replication.

targets.name: GuaB2
targets.significance: Essential for guanine nucleotide biosynthesis; reclassified here from 'central metabolism' since it directly feeds DNA/RNA precursor pools rather than energy metabolism.

targets.name: TrpE
targets.significance: Essential for tryptophan biosynthesis; implicated in intracellular survival during nutrient-limited host conditions.

targets.name: TrpD
targets.significance: Tryptophan pathway; facilitates indole-glycerol-phosphate conversion.

targets.name: AroB
targets.significance: Early shikimate pathway step; required for all downstream aromatic amino acid synthesis.

targets.name: AroG
targets.significance: First committed step of the shikimate pathway, entry point from central carbon metabolism.

targets.name: PheA
targets.significance: Essential for phenylalanine biosynthesis, branching from the shikimate pathway.

targets.name: DapA / DapB
targets.significance: Essential for peptidoglycan cross-linking; meso-DAP is a unique bacterial cell-wall building block absent in humans.

targets.name: LeuA / LeuC / LeuD
targets.significance: Essential for leucine biosynthesis; LeuD auxotrophs used as attenuated vaccine strains.

targets.name: IlvC / IlvD / IlvE
targets.significance: Essential for valine/isoleucine/leucine production; pathway absent in mammals, favorable selectivity window.

targets.name: MetA / MetB / MetC
targets.significance: Essential for sulfur-containing amino acid synthesis.

targets.name: AsnB
targets.significance: Critical for nitrogen metabolism and protein synthesis under nitrogen-limited host conditions.

targets.name: GltD
targets.significance: Central nitrogen assimilation node linking carbon and nitrogen metabolism.

targets.name: PanK
targets.significance: First committed step of coenzyme A biosynthesis; reclassified here from 'amino acid biosynthesis' since CoA is a cofactor, not an amino acid product.

targets.name: RibBA
targets.function: Riboflavin synthase (bifunctional DHBP synthase/GTP cyclohydrolase II)
targets.significance: Essential for vitamin B2 (riboflavin) biosynthesis, precursor to FAD/FMN cofactors.

targets.name: DHPS
targets.significance: Folate biosynthesis pathway enzyme; target of sulfonamides/dapsone-class agents.

targets.name: BioA / BioD
targets.significance: Essential for biotin (vitamin B7) synthesis, an obligate cofactor for carboxylation reactions (e.g. ACCase, relevant to AccD6/mycolic acid synthesis).

targets.name: LipB
targets.significance: See also Central Metabolism category — listed here as well since lipoic acid is formally a cofactor-biosynthesis product; cross-referenced rather than duplicated in data curation.

targets.name: MbtI / MbtA
targets.significance: Essential for iron acquisition during host infection, a well-validated virulence-associated target.

targets.name: CYP121
targets.significance: Essential for cyclodityrosine (mycocyclosin) biosynthesis; validated by genetic essentiality studies.

targets.name: PhoP
targets.significance: Key regulator of virulence lipid biosynthesis (PDIM, SL-1) and ESX-1 secretion; validated attenuation target for live vaccine candidates.

targets.name: RpfA-E
targets.significance: Crucial for reactivation from dormancy/persistence; relevant to relapse and latent TB reactivation biology.
